skill.name: mobile-android-design
skill.description: Guides Android interface design with Material Design 3, Jetpack Compose layouts, navigation patterns, adaptive screens, theming, and accessibility practices.
skill.summary: Design native Android interfaces with Material 3 and Jetpack Compose patterns.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, reconnaissance, and URL patterns. Manual review found these are Markdown code fences, Kotlin UI examples, substring matches, official documentation links, and placeholder deep-link URLs. No evidence found of prompt injection, runtime command execution, credential access, file access, or data exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Static Command Execution Detections Are Markdown Examples
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The external command detections point to fenced Kotlin code examples, not shell execution or runtime hooks. The skill contains Markdown documentation for Compose layouts, navigation, and theming.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited locations are Markdown code fences containing Kotlin snippets. No shell interpreter, process API, or executable script file is present.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are Substring Matches
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The high-severity weak cryptography alerts match words such as design, description, destination, and WindowSizeClass. No cryptographic API or insecure algorithm use was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Targeted review found UI and navigation terminology at the flagged locations. There is no MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, cipher setup, or custom cryptography.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation Links and Placeholders
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. The URL detections are official Android and Material documentation links or placeholder deep-link base paths used in Android navigation examples. They do not perform network requests.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed URLs appear in Markdown resource lists and static Compose deep-link declarations. No fetch, HTTP client, telemetry, or exfiltration behavior was found.

content.value_statement: Android teams need consistent screens that follow current Material and Compose expectations. This skill provides practical patterns for layouts, navigation, theming, components, and adaptive behavior.
actual_capabilities: Explains Material Design 3 principles for Android interfaces.
Provides Jetpack Compose layout patterns for lists, grids, cards, and forms.
Describes Android navigation patterns including bottom bars, drawers, rails, and deep links.
Guides color schemes, dynamic color, typography, elevation, and shape usage.
Covers adaptive layout choices for phones, tablets, foldables, and larger screens.
Highlights accessibility practices such as touch targets and content descriptions.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not run Android Studio or Gradle builds.
Code snippets may need version updates for a specific Compose dependency set.
It does not replace usability testing on real Android devices.
It does not provide a complete application architecture or backend integration.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Android Screen
use_cases.description: Define layout structure, component choices, navigation placement, and responsive behavior before implementation.

use_cases.title: Implement Compose UI Patterns
use_cases.description: Use focused Compose examples for cards, lists, forms, buttons, navigation, and themed surfaces.

use_cases.title: Review Material 3 Consistency
use_cases.description: Check an Android interface against theming, accessibility, navigation, and adaptive layout guidance.

prompt_templates.title: Start an Android Screen
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner planning for one mobile screen.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a Material 3 Android screen for [feature]. Include layout, components, navigation, empty state, and accessibility notes.

prompt_templates.title: Convert Requirements to Compose UI
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn these requirements into a Jetpack Compose UI plan: [requirements]. Include component hierarchy, state, theming, and preview cases.

prompt_templates.title: Audit an Existing Android Flow
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of navigation and interaction quality.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Android flow for Material 3, navigation, accessibility, and adaptive layout issues: [flow description]. Prioritize fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Multi-Form-Factor Experience
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced tablet, foldable, and phone design.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an adaptive Android design strategy for [app area]. Cover compact, medium, and expanded layouts with navigation changes.

output_examples.input: Design a settings screen for an Android finance app.
output: A grouped settings layout using Material 3 list items, switches, and dividers.
Bottom navigation remains visible on phones, while expanded layouts use a navigation rail.
Accessibility notes cover readable labels, 48dp touch targets, and clear account-security actions.

output_examples.input: Review this Compose dashboard for tablet support.
output: The review recommends moving from a single column to a two-pane layout on expanded width.
It suggests replacing bottom navigation with a navigation rail on medium screens.
It flags dense cards, missing content descriptions, and inconsistent theme color usage.

output_examples.input: Create a Material 3 theme checklist for my Android app.
output_examples.output: The response summarizes dynamic color support, light and dark schemes, typography scale, shape rules, elevation use, and contrast checks.

best_practices: Start with the target form factors and choose navigation before detailing individual components.
Use MaterialTheme tokens for color, typography, shape, and spacing decisions.
Check accessibility early, including labels, contrast, touch targets, and screen-reader behavior.
anti_patterns: Do not copy mobile phone layouts directly to tablets without adaptive structure.
Do not hardcode colors when theme tokens can preserve dark mode and dynamic color.
Do not treat example snippets as complete production code without dependency and state review.

faq.question: Does this skill generate complete Android apps?
faq.answer: No. It provides design and Compose UI guidance, not a full project generator.

faq.question: Can it help with Jetpack Compose components?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes patterns for lists, cards, forms, buttons, sheets, dialogs, and navigation surfaces.

faq.question: Does it cover Material Design 3?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers Material 3 principles, dynamic color, typography, shapes, elevation, and component usage.

faq.question: Is it useful for tablet and foldable design?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes adaptive layout guidance using compact, medium, and expanded screen classes.

faq.question: Does it execute code or access project files?
faq.answer: No. The skill is Markdown documentation and examples. It does not include executable scripts.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
